synopsis: This concise history surveys major episodes of financial speculation over three centuries, from early market manias to junk bonds. It explains how recurring faith in novelty, collective pursuit of profit, and short financial memory create bubbles whose collapse repeats familiar patterns.

sources.title: A Short History of Financial Euphoria by John Kenneth Galbraith | Penguin Random House

synopsis: This anthology presents Edwin Curley’s translation of Spinoza’s Ethics alongside selections from the Treatise on the Emendation of the Intellect and other writings. The texts examine God, nature, mind and body, knowledge, freedom, emotion, and the philosophical path toward human well-being.

sources.title: A Spinoza reader: the Ethics and other works | WorldCat

synopsis: This collection gathers seven essays on television, professional tennis, the Illinois State Fair, David Lynch’s films, literary theory, and commercial entertainment. Its title piece chronicles a Caribbean luxury cruise, examining the managed pleasures, excesses, and unease built into the experience.

sources.title: A Supposedly Fun Thing I’ll Never Do Again | Hachette Book Group

synopsis: The book proposes that cortical columns use maplike reference frames to build many parallel models of objects and environments, collectively producing perception and intelligence. It applies this framework to consciousness, the sense of self, learning, and the design and possible consequences of genuinely intelligent machines.

sources.title: A Thousand Brains by Jeff Hawkins | Hachette Book Group

synopsis: The book argues that American housing, energy, infrastructure, science, and public services suffer from policy-created scarcity and an eroded capacity to build. It traces regulatory, political, and institutional barriers to action and proposes a governing agenda focused on increasing supply, improving state capacity, and enabling material progress.

Fifteen schoolboys aboard a schooner drift away from Auckland, survive a violent Pacific storm, and beach their damaged vessel on an uninhabited island. Over two years, they organize shelter and food, navigate rivalries within the group, confront outside dangers, and work toward rescue.

sources.title: Adrift in the Pacific by Jules Verne | Google Books
Adrift in the Pacific: Two Years Holiday | Open Library

synopsis: This textbook explains digestion, absorption, transport, and metabolism of carbohydrates, fats, and proteins, linking biochemical pathways to normal human physiology. It also covers vitamins, minerals, energy balance, fluid and electrolyte regulation, nutrient deficiency and toxicity, and nutrition’s role in chronic disease.

sources.title: Advanced Nutrition and Human Metabolism, 6th Edition | Cengage

synopsis: This guide translates fundamental investment ideas into portfolio construction and risk-management decisions using practical quantitative tools. It covers position sizing, factor models, performance and risk decomposition, hedging, diversification, loss and tail-risk controls, leverage, and adapting techniques as strategies grow.

sources.title: Advanced Portfolio Management: A Quant’s Guide for Fundamental Investors | Wiley

synopsis: The book rereads early Buddhist texts to reconstruct the historical Buddha as a pragmatic ethicist rather than a dogmatic metaphysician. Through accounts of members of his inner circle, it develops a secular ethical, contemplative, and philosophical framework in which dharma remains an evolving culture of awakening.
